Title: Comprehensive Genome‐Wide Identification and Expression Analysis of the N6‐Methyladenosine (m6A) Regulatory Network Influences Rapid Stress Adaptation With Exogenous Melatonin in Rice

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-responsive m6A writer candidate upregulated under drought, salt, and melatonin-associated treatments
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide identification; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR under abiotic stress, melatonin, and hormone treatments
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in seedling, leaf, and stem; induced by drought at 12 h and salt at 24 h; increased under melatonin with drought/salt at 24 h
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought; salt; exogenous melatonin; jasmonic acid; salicylic acid; gibberellic acid
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets in Supplementary Tables S1 and S2
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts in m6A RNA methylation pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMTA-1 is a nuclear MT-A70 family writer. 2. It is induced by drought and salt stress in a time-dependent manner. 3. Exogenous melatonin enhanced its expression under drought and salt conditions.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate component of m6A machinery for stress-resilience engineering, pending direct functional validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct stress-tolerance mutant or transgenic phenotype shown.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-responsive m6A writer candidate significantly upregulated under salt and heat stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower in several tissues; significantly upregulated by 150 mM NaCl at 24 and 48 h; also induced by heat and salinity at 24 h
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low/absent in several tissues but stress inducible
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt; heat; jasmonic acid; salicylic acid
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets in Supplementary Tables S1 and S2
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts in m6A RNA methylation pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMTA-2 is a nuclear writer-family gene with limited basal tissue expression. 2. It is strongly induced by NaCl treatment at 24–48 h. 3. It also responds to heat stress.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stress-responsive epitranscriptomic target for rice improvement
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct causal phenotype or biochemical validation shown.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-responsive m6A reader candidate induced by cold, salt, drought, and melatonin-drought treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under cold, salt, and drought by RNA-seq; pronounced response to MT-D after 48 h by qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold; salt; drought; exogenous melatonin with drought; salicylic acid
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets in Supplementary Tables S1 and S2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsECT-9 is a YTH-domain reader expressed in stress contexts. 2. It is induced by cold, salt, and drought. 3. It shows a significant phase-specific response to melatonin plus drought at 48 h.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for stress-adaptation engineering in rice
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as responsive, but not functionally dissected as deeply as OsECT2.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ALKBH family; 2OG-Fe(II) oxygenase m6A eraser candidate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-responsive m6A eraser candidate strongly induced by cold, heat, drought, and salt stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced after 24 h cold and heat stress; also responds under drought and salt
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets in Supplementary Tables S1 and S2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsALKBH-32 is an ALKBH-family eraser candidate. 2. It shows strong induction under extreme temperature stress. 3. It also responds to drought and salt, indicating a role in stress-associated methylation homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate demethylase target for improving tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Localization is in silico prediction only; no direct enzymatic assay reported.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ALKBH family; 2OG-Fe(II) oxygenase m6A eraser candidate
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-responsive m6A eraser candidate induced by drought and melatonin-drought treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under drought after 48 h; significant upregulation during MT-D at 12 h; expression reduced under salt relative to control in some comparisons
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought; salt; exogenous melatonin with drought; gibberellic acid; salicylic acid
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets in Supplementary Tables S1 and S2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsALKBH-42 is a cytoplasmic ALKBH-family eraser candidate. 2. It is induced by prolonged drought stress. 3. It is also significantly induced by melatonin plus drought at early time points.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating stress-responsive m6A homeostasis under drought
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Responses to GA and SA were not significant.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: m6A-modified mRNAs; predicted METTL14-like writer module-associated transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: m6A recognition/binding likely affecting mRNA fate and post-transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salt stress; exogenous melatonin; jasmonic acid; ABA-related promoter inputs inferred from ABRE motifs; cold-responsive promoter inputs inferred from LTR motifs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key melatonin-responsive m6A reader implicated as a central hub in salt-stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rapid salt-stress adaptation/tolerance in rice in association with exogenous melatonin-responsive expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide identification; promoter cis-element analysis; RNA-seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR under stress/hormone treatments; STRING PPI analysis; AlphaFold/PyMOL structural prediction; GFP-based transient expression in rice protoplasts for subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Time-specific induction under salt stress; significant upregulation at 24 h NaCl; MT-S-induced upregulation at 24 h; JA responsive; non-significant under some cold and MT-D time points
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in multiple rice tissues; stress inducible, especially under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Supported by PPI network association with METTL14-like writer proteins and RNA-binding factors
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for OsECT2-GFP localization assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt (150 mM NaCl); drought; cold; heat; exogenous melatonin (100 μM); jasmonic acid (100 μM); salicylic acid (100 μM); gibberellic acid (100 μM)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from rice tissues and abiotic stress datasets retrieved from Rice Genome Annotation Project/NCBI SRA; accession numbers listed in Supplementary Tables S1 and S2
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: m6A epitranscriptomic regulation; OsECT2 functions as reader of m6A-modified RNAs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsECT2 was identified as a YTH-domain m6A reader with conserved m6A-binding residues and predicted aromatic cage. 2. OsECT2 is predominantly nuclear by GFP localization in rice protoplasts. 3. Its promoter is enriched in stress- and ABA-related cis-elements, supporting multi-stress responsiveness. 4. OsECT2 is significantly induced by salt stress and by melatonin under salt stress in a time-dependent manner. 5. PPI analysis places OsECT2 as a central hub interacting with METTL14-like writer proteins and RNA-binding factors, supporting a coordinated epitranscriptomic role in salt adaptation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering or marker-assisted improvement of melatonin-responsive salt tolerance in rice; should be validated by direct functional genetics before deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although the paper concludes OsECT2 is a crucial regulator of salt adaptation, direct knockout/overexpression phenotypic validation was not presented in this article.
